title: NEO GENESIS EVANGELION NGE review from 1997
display_title: NEO GENESIS EVANGELION NGE review from 1997
wikitext:
[]: NEO GENESIS EVANGELION (NGE "review" from 1997)             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Sat Apr 02, 2011 5:13 pm Found this hilarious little gem while going through my old stuff. It's an article about NGE from a 1997 issue of the now defunct Polish anime magazine "Kawaii". (As far as I can tell, it's their first material on Eva. They did get a lot better later.) Translation by me, keeping the style and... creativity. Brace yourselves. NEO GENESIS EVANGELION This series has been successful in the global anime market for some time now, and in Japan it was considered the most popular series of 1996. As the Japanese themselves claim, this is the first such hugely successful series since the premiere of Sailor Moon in 1990. Evangelion has not come down from the top of the charts for several months now, it's still going strong, which should come as no surprise considering it's another series from the studio GAINAX. So far, 5 of the 24 OAVs intended for video distribution have been released. The story alludes to a prophecy contained in the ancient "Book of Giant Robots", which begins with a great disaster - a meteor impact in the year 2015, which causes the annihilation of over half of the Earth's denizens. After the great explosion our planet is attacked by alien invaders called Angels, who rather successfully murder nearly the entire remaining population. Only a handful of individuals survives in an old section of Tokyo-3, where they set up a secret organisation which leads guerilla warfare against the Angels. Thanks to the possession of an advanced high-tech factory - "NERV", the humans succeed to build biomechanical robots codenamed "Eva", which are the only weapon capable of facing the mighty invaders. The protagonist of the show is the young Shinji Icarus - a teenager who is one of the first to be allowed to "ride" the Evangelion. It is his character that we usually accompany during the spectacular fight scenes, trying with him to solve a multitude of amazing mysteries. While watching the show, one frequently gets the impression of actually being one of the pilots of the biorobots. The show's creators managed perfectly to entangle the viewer into the plot of the movie, giving him the opportunity to experience amazing feelings, almost the same as those of the protagonists. Just like them, we'll pay any price to get answers to the key questions of the show: "why was the Earth attacked at all?", or "why were all Evangelion pilots born 9 months before the meteor impact?". What's most gripping about the movie are the glorious transformantions of people and robots, stunningly perfect spectacular fights of the Eva-biorobots against the Angels and a multitude of confusing super-secret machinations tangled into the plot of an investigation, the complications of which are only comparable to X-Files! In the further episodes, the character designs get markedly better, and the plot really gets into full swing. Bizarre, often highly brutal and shocking scenes start appearing, leading to much controversy among the viewers. With its atmosphere and the message contained within, the fillm is practically begging for a boycott, often leading to disgust among the audience. The huge success of the Evangelion OAV series encouraged the producers to create the third feature film yet. It premiered this summer and (what's hardly surprising), became another blockbuster. But that's not all! Evangelion also appeared on the Sega Saturn platform, and the studio followed up with an expansion into the computer market, giving a nice present to Macintosh and PC fans. "Neo Genesis Evangelion" was released as a five-disc CD-ROM version, and with two more discs planned for release before the end of August.

[]: Re: NEO GENESIS EVANGELION (NGE "review" from 1997             Quote Post by Stryker » Sat Apr 02, 2011 5:19 pm Kodaemon wrote: The story alludes to a prophecy contained in the ancient "Book of Giant Robots", which begins with a great disaster - a meteor impact in the year 2015, which causes the annihilation of over half of the Earth's denizens.

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Sat Apr 02, 2011 5:32 pm The polish rendering of the name Icarus is Ikar, so that's a difference of just one letter ;).

[]: Re: NEO GENESIS EVANGELION (NGE "review" from 1997             Quote Post by symbv » Sat Apr 02, 2011 5:36 pm Kodaemon wrote: Found this hilarious little gem while going through my old stuff. It's an article about NGE from a 1997 issue of the now defunct Polish anime magazine "Kawaii". (As far as I can tell, it's their first material on Eva. They did get a lot better later.) Translation by me, keeping the style and... creativity. Brace yourselves.

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Sat Apr 02, 2011 5:48 pm The guy who wrote the article got fired a few months later. The magazine benefitted greatly ;)

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Sat Apr 02, 2011 6:18 pm Damn, been a while since I've got to use this one...

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sat Apr 02, 2011 11:14 pm Where can I find this "book of giant robots" I wonder? And also this mythical third NGE movie (maybe they're counting D&R as two seperate movies?)

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Apr 02, 2011 11:15 pm Trajan wrote: Where can I find this "book of giant robots" I wonder?

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Sun Apr 03, 2011 12:37 am Article: Trajan wrote: Where can I find this "book of giant robots" I wonder? And also this mythical third NGE movie (maybe they're counting D&R as two seperate movies?)

[]:             Quote Post by Combs » Sun Apr 03, 2011 1:38 am An odd kinda marker for progress in Eastern Europe isn't it? I'd love to go there someday.

[]: Re: NEO GENESIS EVANGELION (NGE "review" from 1997             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Apr 03, 2011 4:03 am It... became another blockbuster. But that's not all! Evangelion also appeared on the Sega Saturn platform

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Fri Apr 08, 2011 12:51 pm the complications of which are only comparable to X-Files!

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Fri Apr 08, 2011 2:26 pm tomrule123 wrote: ... no comment. (...ok, I don't watch X-Files, but I'm not sure if comparing this to the mindf*** show would do justice.)

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Fri Apr 08, 2011 2:34 pm Stryker wrote: Actually, it surprisingly does. X-files give a mind full of fuck, and I fully suggest you check it out. Although, sometimes its pretty scary (it's pretty weird too, but I don't think that needs mentioning).

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Apr 08, 2011 7:13 pm a teenager who is one of the first to be allowed to "ride" the Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Fri Apr 08, 2011 8:39 pm "Shinji Icarus" has a nice ring to it. And it was Icarus' father who warned him not to fly too close to the sun.

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Sun Apr 10, 2011 12:46 pm I wonder where this guy got his information from, besides from his behind. Notice how he says the bare minimum about the series. He mentions angels, impacts, biorobots, mysteries, Nerv, and Shinji Icarus. Other than that, he tells us very little about the series other than it's "MYSTERIOUS AND ACTION PACKED, AND BEGGING FOR A BOYCOTT LAWL."

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Sun Apr 10, 2011 1:53 pm Hey, if I read that review with no previous knowledge of Eva, I'd sure as hell want to watch it.

[]:             Quote Post by GoGoGengar » Thu Apr 21, 2011 6:08 pm I doubt anyone is that stupid xD Considering he's polish he probably got some misleading subtitles. That's the only explanation
